caveat: Checks the artifact FLOW and signature THRESHOLDS the layout declares, given already-verified link signatures — it does NOT perform cryptographic signature verification or key management, which are the caller's layer (pair with a signing/PKI stack). Correctness is demonstrated over hand-built rule cases and one end-to-end layout with five tamperings, not proven for every layout; a behaviour no artifact rule anticipates passes, so the trailing DISALLOW catch-all matters.
statement: The vendored in-toto artifact-rule engine passes all 20 checks with 0 mismatches: the SEVEN artifact-rule types (MATCH, CREATE, DELETE, MODIFY, ALLOW, DISALLOW, REQUIRE) each match their spec-derived PASS/FAIL verdict over 14 hand-built cases — including rule-ORDER sensitivity (ALLOW-then-DISALLOW passes what DISALLOW-first rejects) — the canonical clone -> build -> package layout verifies whole (clean_layout_ok = 1), and 5 independent tamperings (an unauthorized functionary, a below-threshold signature count, an un-sourced product, a cross-step MATCH hash mismatch, and a DISALLOW-guarded stray file) are each detected (tamperings_detected = 5), with 6/6 malformed inputs rejected.
